description: Mutating UIKit views or published SwiftUI state from a background thread, causing nondeterministic crashes and UI corruption.
body: Failure mode: a network or background-queue completion handler updates a view or observable state directly without hopping to the main thread, so UIKit's main-thread-only contract is violated. Detection signals: the Main Thread Checker logs purple warnings; intermittent crashes or visual glitches that depend on timing; bugs that vanish under the debugger. Do not touch UI off the main thread. Safer fallback: do the work in the background, then dispatch the UI update to the main queue or a @MainActor context, and annotate UI-driving types @MainActor so the compiler enforces it.
